pages.rankField: strictGlobalRank computed as Alpha Score descending, ticker tie-break, matching app/latent-ai-nodes/page.tsx

methodology.objective: Evaluate current alpha ranking relevance for hidden/under-owned upside in latent AI infrastructure, power/electrical, sensors, connectors, edge infrastructure, scaling-law beneficiaries, and AI 2027 compute-demand beneficiaries as of 2026-06-08.
methodology.rankMoveRule: For each page, recomputed an implied rank from the page's current AI2027/price-adjusted score, sorting higher adjusted score first and smaller market cap as a tie-break. Broad and strict use an 8-rank materiality threshold; scaling uses a 4-rank threshold. Moves are up/hold/down by displayed rank versus implied rank. Remove is reserved for names no longer investable as standalone public alpha candidates or severe corporate-status failures.
methodology.valuationInputs: Market cap/YTD inputs are the repo market-data refresh dated 2026-06-03 where same-day 2026-06-08 quote access was unavailable/rate-limited.
methodology.valuationAdjustments: YTD run-ups above roughly 90% cap up moves to hold; above roughly 175% bias to down. Scaling names above roughly 250% YTD are down. Mega-cap names above about $50B are capped because hidden alpha is lower; above about $100B with a major run-up can be down despite strategic relevance.
methodology.evidenceInputs: Used local page source maps plus web-opened current/primary sources where feasible: IEA, Morgan Stanley, CBRE, S&P Global, McKinsey, official investor-relations pages/releases for Belden, EnerSys, Volex, Hammond Power, Voltamp, TOWA, Tekscend, Powell, Aehr, Modine, Wolfspeed, and FARO/AMETEK.
methodology.caveats: This is ranking-triage, not investment advice or a price target. Some international micro/small caps have limited same-day quote availability; sourceUrls preserve the evidence trail for follow-up diligence.
